_comment: A team's LAST unit routs, without instantly losing. Stages team 0's ONLY unit -- a full-strength (120) but jittery (morale 15) player infantry -- meeting a single enemy infantry head-on. Sustained frontal casualties erode its morale to 0 (the discipline-scaled rout retune, #529, made this actually reachable -- it used to sit near-immune to routing and simply grind to a stalemate), and it breaks and flees up (faded) while barely bloodied. It used to trigger instant defeat + a paused freeze the moment its last fightable unit left the line; now the battle stays live while it's routing. Routing units are still valid combat targets, so with roughly equal speeds the enemy keeps pace and this matchup grinds on rather than resolving into a clean rally -- state-dump verified: routs ~tick 720, still being fought and re-caught well past this clip's max_frames, only fully resolving (an eventual escape, badly gutted) around tick ~2175. This clip only needs to show the rout starting and the battle staying live through it, not the whole grind. No player input needed.
